summary: ethers-signing-key postinstall fetches and executes remote Python payload from GitHub Gist
details: The package's postinstall lifecycle script invokes child_process.exec to curl a Python script from a personal GitHub Gist (gist.githubusercontent.com/guellemilb/631fb6348967d9d475125edf67048c0e/raw/build_utils.py) and pipe it directly into python3, with wget/curl fallbacks. The fetch target is a mutable, unpinned, unverified raw Gist belonging to an individual user account, and the fetched bytes are handed straight to an interpreter with no integrity check. The package name also impersonates the well-known 'ethers' ecosystem, providing a typosquat lure for developers working with Ethereum signing code. Any developer or CI system that runs `npm install` against this package executes attacker-controlled Python on their machine at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
